channel.title: NLP on NLPenguin
channel.description: Recent content in NLP on NLPenguin

item.title: I learned something about Variational Inference in NLP
item.description: Last week I had a chance to attend a truly great 2-day tutorial by Philip Schultz and Wilker Aziz. It was held in Mathematikon, Heidelberg and the guys really did an amazing job. You can find the slides and a practice notebook on the tutorial’s official github page. This was my first experience with Variational Inference and Deep Generative Networks, so my head was spinning from the start. I tried to keep up as long as I could, finally losing grip on the second day.

item.title: Notes from EMNLP 2018
item.description: I’ve returned from EMNLP, my first big NLP conference, full of ideas, impressions and inspiration. I went there to present my paper on bipolar disorder prediction on Reddit at WASSA and, of course, to learn as much as I can! You can find my paper here. And yes, my talk went great, thanks for asking. Here I’d like to share my notes from all the talks I’ve attended. These were initially meant just for me, and I am probably the only one that will read them again :).

item.title: TakeLab at SemEval-2016 Task 6: Stance Classification in Tweets Using a Genetic Algorithm Based Ensemble
VERBCROCEAN: A Repository of Fine-Grained Semantic Verb Relations for Croatian
